content:
CHURN REPORT
Customer: Crestline Manufacturing
Customer ID: C004
Contract ID: K004
Account Executive: Tom Rivera
Contract End Date: May 31, 2024
Report Date: June 10, 2024

REASON FOR CHURN
Crestline Manufacturing chose not to renew their DataSync Pro Starter contract at the end of May 2024. The primary reason cited was low platform adoption among their factory floor managers. Despite onboarding sessions conducted in Q3 2023, fewer than 4 of the 12 licensed users were logging in regularly by Q1 2024.

CONTRIBUTING FACTORS
During the March 2024 renewal call, operations manager Dale Pruitt stated that the team found the interface too complex for their day-to-day workflows. They were primarily looking for a simpler scheduling and inventory tool, which DataSync Pro was not designed to replace. Tom Rivera offered a downgrade to a lower user count and a 15% discount to retain the account, but Crestline declined.

A secondary factor was budget pressure. Crestline had recently invested in new CNC equipment and was trimming SaaS spend across departments. The software was seen as a non-essential line item by their CFO.

COMPETITIVE LOSS
No direct competitor was named. Crestline indicated they would revert to using spreadsheets and their existing ERP system for the workflows DataSync Pro had been covering.

LESSONS LEARNED
This account may have been a poor fit from the start. The use case was narrower than our platform's strengths. Future deals in the manufacturing vertical should include a more rigorous discovery process to confirm workflow alignment before closing.

FINAL ARR LOST: $6,480
